items.desc_en: Okinawa designated cultural property: オホホ(舞踊・狂言の部). Category: 市町村指定無形民俗文化財. Type: その他. Location: 竹富町.
items.desc_ja: 竹富町の市町村指定無形民俗文化財（その他）

items.title_en: Eisa: Obon Ancestor Dance with Drums
items.title_ja: エイサー：盂蘭盆会の太鼓踊り
items.desc_en: Performed during Obon (July 13-15 lunar calendar) to welcome and send off ancestral spirits. Youth groups (seinen-kai) from each village perform processions with paranku (hand drums), odaiko (large drums), and shimedaiko. Regional styles vary dramatically: Koza, Chibana, Heshikiya each have distinct
items.desc_ja: 旧暦7月13-15日の盂蘭盆会に祖先の霊を迎え送る踊り。各集落の青年会がパランクー・大太鼓・締太鼓で練り歩く。コザ・知花・平敷屋など地域ごとに様式が異なる。

items.title_ja: 組踊：琉球音楽劇（ユネスコ無形文化遺産）
items.desc_en: Theatrical art form created by Tamagusuku Chokun in 1719 for Chinese investiture envoys. Combines Ryukyuan music (sanshin, koten ongaku), dance, and spoken drama. Five classical plays form the core repertoire: Nidou Tekiuchi, Shushin Kaneiri, Mekarushi, Koko no Maki, Onna Monogurui. UNESCO Intangibl
items.desc_ja: 1719年に玉城朝薫が中国冊封使歓待のために創作した総合舞台芸術。琉球音楽・舞踊・台詞劇を融合。二童敵討・執心鐘入・銘苅子・孝行の巻・女物狂の五番が古典。2010年ユネスコ無形文化遺産。

items.title_en: Sanshin: Three-stringed Lute and Musical Heritage
items.title_ja: 三線：三弦楽器と音楽遺産
items.desc_en: Central instrument of Okinawan music, derived from the Chinese sanxian via the Ryukyu Kingdom. The body is traditionally covered with python skin (now often synthetic). Three tuning systems: honchoshi, niage, sansage. Mastery involves not only technique but knowledge of classical song cycles (koten
items.desc_ja: 沖縄音楽の中核楽器。中国三弦から琉球王国経由で伝来。胴は伝統的にニシキヘビ革。本調子・二揚・三下げの3調弦法。古典音楽の歌曲群と儀礼的文脈の習得を含む。

items.title_en: Ryukyu Buyo: Classical Dance Categories and Repertoire
items.title_ja: 琉球舞踊：古典舞踊の分類と演目
items.desc_en: Classical dance tradition divided into three categories: zo-odori (men's dance with elegant, measured movements), wakashu-odori (youth dance), and onna-odori (women's dance). Each category has specific repertoire, costume requirements, and movement vocabularies rooted in court culture. Designated Im
items.desc_ja: 三分類の古典舞踊：老踊（優雅で緩やかな男性舞踊）、若衆踊（若者舞踊）、女踊（女性舞踊）。各分類に固有の演目・衣装・動作語彙があり宮廷文化に根ざす。重要無形文化財。

items.title_en: Kachashi: Celebration Dance and Communal Joy Expression
items.title_ja: カチャーシー：祝いの踊りと共同歓喜表現
items.desc_en: Spontaneous celebratory dance performed at the climax of festivals, weddings, and gatherings. Characterized by raised hands waving overhead with improvised movements. Serves as social bonding mechanism transcending status, age, and gender. The tempo accelerates through the performance, building coll
items.desc_ja: 祭り・結婚式・宴会のクライマックスで踊る即興的祝いの踊り。頭上で手を振る動作が特徴。地位・年齢・性別を超えた社会的結合装置。テンポが加速し集合的歓喜を醸成。

items.desc_en: Traditional circular formation for Eisa dance featuring synchronized movements. Dancers arrange in concentric circles, creating rhythmic patterns. Essential during Obon festivals, combining spiritual veneration with community bonding and ancestral commemoration practices.
items.desc_ja: エイサーの基本的な円形陣形。複数の踊り手が同心円状に配置され、統一された動きを創出。盆踊りの中核をなし、精神的儀式と共同体の結束を実現する伝統的な形式。

items.desc_en: Classical Ryukyuan musical drama blending dance, music, and spoken narrative. Tells historical and mythological stories through integrated performance. Requires extensive training in movement vocabulary, vocal techniques, and dramatic interpretation for authentic transmission.
items.desc_ja: 琉球古典劇の統合的表現形式。踊り、音楽、台詞を組み合わせて物語を展開。歴史的・神話的題材を扱い、高度な修練を要する伝統芸能の最高峰とされている。

items.desc_en: Specialized finger-picking and strumming techniques for traditional three-stringed instrument. Encompasses various regional styles and tuning systems. Mastery requires years of dedicated practice, precise hand positioning, and deep understanding of Ryukyuan musical scales and expressions.
items.desc_ja: 三線独特の弾奏法技術。複数の地域的奏法様式と調弦法が存在。習得には長年の修練が必要で、手の位置や琉球音階への深い理解が不可欠である。

items.desc_en: Codified classical dance style with refined movements and specific aesthetic principles. Emphasizes graceful arm positions, deliberate footwork, and emotional expression. Transmitted through formal schools maintaining distinct repertoires and teaching methodologies within Ryukyuan cultural framework
items.desc_ja: 琉球舞踊の最高峰的形式。洗練された身体表現と特定の美学原理に基づく。腕の位置、足の運び、感情表現が厳密に定められ、各流派が独自の教授法を維持している。

items.desc_en: Spontaneous hand-clapping dance performed at celebrations and social gatherings. Features improvised movements synchronized with rhythmic clapping patterns. Highly accessible form enabling community participation, strengthening social bonds during festive occasions and ceremonial events.
items.desc_ja: 祝いの席での即興的手踊り。手拍子のリズムに合わせた自由な動き。誰でも参加でき、共同体の結束を強化する民間芸能の最も親しみやすい形式である。

items.desc_en: Elaborate lion costume creation involving specialized construction techniques and symbolic decoration. Incorporates traditional materials and specific color patterns representing protective spiritual forces. Transmitted through craft lineages maintaining knowledge of material selection and assembly
items.desc_ja: 獅子舞用衣装の製作技術。伝統的材料と象徴的な色彩装飾を使用。保護的な精神力を表現する特定のパターンを持つ。工芸的知識は家族系統を通じて伝承される。

items.title_ja: エイサーの太鼓リズム
items.desc_en: Complex polyrhythmic patterns performed on traditional drums during Eisa performances. Establishes temporal structure coordinating multiple dancers and musicians. Knowledge encompasses regional variations and seasonal modifications, transmitted orally through community practice and experienced drumm
items.desc_ja: エイサーで用いられる複合的なリズムパターン。複数の踊り手と楽器奏者の協調を生み出す時間的構造。地域的変異と季節的変更を含む知識は口頭伝承される。

items.desc_en: Collection of traditional plays preserving Ryukyuan historical narratives and cultural values. Each performance conveys specific moral lessons and social hierarchies through carefully choreographed scenes. Requires comprehensive memorization of dialogue, movement sequences, and musical accompaniment
items.desc_ja: 琉球の歴史と文化的価値を保存する古典劇レパートリー。各演目は特定の道徳的教訓と社会的階級制度を表現。台詞、身体表現、音楽の総合的修得が必須。

items.desc_en: Integrated musical support for classical dance utilizing traditional instruments. Musicians coordinate rhythmic patterns with dancers' movements creating unified artistic expression. Requires intimate knowledge of dance repertoire and sensitive responsiveness to performers' emotional intentions and
items.desc_ja: 琉球舞踊に不可欠な音楽伴奏。伝統楽器奏者が踊り手の動きと密接に協調。舞踊レパートリーへの深い理解と表現者の感情や時間的ニュアンスへの敏感な対応が必要。

items.desc_en: Dynamic movement vocabulary for lion dance character performance. Incorporates stylized gaits, acrobatic elements, and expressive head movements conveying supernatural energy. Performers undergo specialized training developing physical control, spatial awareness, and understanding of spiritual signi
items.desc_ja: 獅子舞特有の身体表現技法。様式化された歩行、アクロバット的要素、表現的な頭部の動き。超自然的エネルギーを表現。舞踊者には高度な身体制御と精神的意義の理解が要求される。

items.title_ja: エイサーの円陣踊り技法
items.desc_en: Eisa circle dance formations teach synchronized group movement patterns fundamental to Okinawan festival celebrations. Dancers learn timing, spatial awareness, and collective rhythmic coordination essential for traditional Bon festival performances.
items.desc_ja: エイサーの円陣踊り形成は、沖縄祭りの基本となる集団同期動作パターンを教える。タイミング、空間認識、盆祭り公演に必須の集団リズム調整を習得する。

items.title_ja: 組踊りの古典物語振付
items.desc_en: Kumiodori integrates narrative storytelling with choreographed movement, preserving historical tales through performed drama. Practitioners learn character portrayal, emotional expression, and classical Ryukyuan dance vocabulary embedded in narrative contexts.
items.desc_ja: 組踊りは物語語りと振付運動を統合し、歴史的物語を演技で保存する。古典琉球舞踊の語彙と感情表現、人物描写を習得する伝統劇芸能。

items.title_ja: 三線の調弦と保守技術
items.desc_en: Sanshin maintenance knowledge encompasses traditional tuning methods, string replacement, and instrument preservation techniques. Craftspeople learn wood selection, binding methods, and acoustic properties essential for maintaining instrument quality and sound.
items.desc_ja: 三線保守知識は伝統的調弦法、弦交換、楽器保存技術を含む。木材選定、結合方法、音響特性を習得する職人的知識である。

items.title_en: Ryukyu Buyo Hand and Arm Movement Vocabulary
items.title_ja: 琉球舞踊の手足運動語彙
items.desc_en: Ryukyu buyo teaches distinctive hand and arm movements representing specific meanings and emotions. Dancers learn flowing wrist rotations, finger positions, and arm gestures conveying narratives central to Okinawan aesthetic expression.
items.desc_ja: 琉球舞踊は特定の意味と感情を表現する手足運動を教える。流動的な手首回転、指位置、腕動作が沖縄美学表現の中心をなす。

items.title_ja: カチャーシーの参加型祝い踊り
items.desc_en: Kachashi teaches spontaneous, inclusive circle dancing performed at celebrations and festivals. Participants learn improvisation, joy expression, and community participation rituals that democratize traditional dance, welcoming all skill levels.
items.desc_ja: カチャーシーは祝い祭りで行う即興的な円陣踊りを教える。即興性、喜びの表現、全技能レベルを歓迎する共同体参加儀式である。

items.title_en: Shishi-Mai Lion Dance Costume and Mask Crafting
items.title_ja: 獅子舞の衣装と面製作技術
items.desc_en: Shishi-mai mask and costume creation preserves specialized crafting techniques for lion head construction and fabric design. Artisans learn traditional materials, coloring methods, and construction processes maintaining authentic aesthetic standards.
items.desc_ja: 獅子舞面と衣装製作は獅子頭構築と生地設計の専門技術を保存する。材料、着色法、真正性維持の構築方法を習得する職人技術。

items.title_en: Eisa Drum Rhythm Patterns and Percussion Techniques
items.title_ja: エイサー太鼓リズムと打楽器技法
items.desc_en: Eisa drumming teaches traditional percussion patterns, striking techniques, and rhythm variations essential for festival performances. Drummers master hand coordination, dynamic control, and rhythmic variation creating energetic soundscapes.
items.desc_ja: エイサー太鼓は伝統的打楽器パターンと打撃技法を教える。手の協調性、動的コントロール、リズム変化で活動的音景を創造する。

items.title_en: Sanshin Song Repertoire and Musical Interpretation
items.title_ja: 三線の歌唱レパートリーと音楽解釈
items.desc_en: Sanshin musicians learn traditional song collections, vocal techniques, and emotional interpretation within Okinawan musical contexts. Practitioners develop understanding of regional variations, historical compositions, and expressive performance standards.
items.desc_ja: 三線奏者は伝統歌曲集、声楽技法、沖縄音楽的感情解釈を学ぶ。地域変異、歴史的作品、表現的演奏基準の理解を習得する。

items.title_en: Shishi-Mai Lion Head Movement and Animation
items.title_ja: 獅子舞の頭部運動と躍動表現
items.desc_en: Shishi-mai performers learn coordinated head movements, jaw articulation, and expressive animation techniques bringing lion masks to life. Practitioners master timing, spatial navigation, and interaction with audiences creating magical performances.
items.desc_ja: 獅子舞演者は協調的頭部運動、顎操作、獅子を生かす躍動表現技法を学ぶ。観客相互作用で魔法的公演を創造する専門技術である。

items.title_en: Kumiodori Costume and Stage Design Aesthetics
items.title_ja: 組踊りの衣装と舞台設計美学
items.desc_en: Kumiodori stage production encompasses costume design, color symbolism, and spatial arrangement reflecting classical Ryukyuan aesthetics. Practitioners learn visual storytelling, character identification through costume, and stage composition principles.
items.desc_ja: 組踊り舞台制作は古典琉球美学を反映した衣装設計、色彩象徴性、空間配置を含む。視覚的物語、衣装による人物特定、舞台構成原理を習得する。

items.title_ja: エイサーの隊形パターン
items.desc_en: Complex geometric formations used in eisa performances, including circle arrangements and linear processions. Dancers synchronize movements through spatial relationships, creating visual harmony while maintaining rhythmic unity.
items.desc_ja: エイサーの円形隊形や行進列など複雑な幾何学的パターン。踊り手が空間的関係を通じて動きを同期させ、視覚的調和を創出しながら拍子の統一を保つ。

items.desc_en: Distinctive storytelling framework combining dance, music, and dialogue. Kumiodori scripts follow classical Okinawan themes with three-act structures, emphasizing moral lessons through dramatic interplay between characters.
items.desc_ja: 踊り、音楽、台詞を組み合わせた物語構成。古典的琉球テーマに従う三幕構成で、人物間の劇的相互作用を通じて道徳的教訓を強調する組踊の台本形式。

items.desc_en: Specialized methods for adjusting and maintaining sanshin string tension to achieve proper pitch and tonal quality. Techniques include tuning adjustments, string replacement rituals, and tension maintenance during performances.
items.desc_ja: 適切な音高と音色を実現するための三線の弦緊張調整技法。チューニング調整、弦交換儀式、演奏中の緊張保持などの特殊な技術を含む。

items.title_ja: 琉球舞踊の腰の動き語彙
items.desc_en: Sophisticated hip and torso movements fundamental to Ryukyu buyo aesthetics. Distinguished by flowing, undulating motions that contrast with upper body restraint, creating the characteristic graceful silhouette of classical Okinawan dance.
items.desc_ja: 琉球舞踊の美学に基本的な腰と胴体の動き。上半身の抑制と対比される流動的で波打つ動きが特徴で、古典沖縄舞踊の優雅なシルエットを作る。

items.title_en: Noro Priestess System: Village-appointed Female Ritualists
items.title_ja: ノロ制度：集落任命の女性祭司
items.desc_en: Official female priestesses appointed by the Ryukyu Kingdom to oversee village rituals. Each mura (village) had a designated noro who conducted ceremonies at the village utaki, managed the ritual calendar, and served as intermediary between the community and the spirit world. The system was formaliz
items.desc_ja: 琉球王国が集落祭祀を管掌するために任命した公式女性祭司。各村にノロが配置され、御嶽での祭祀、祭祀暦の管理、共同体と霊界の仲介を担った。尚真王代に制度化。

items.title_en: Kikoe-ogimi: Supreme Priestess of the Ryukyu Kingdom
items.title_ja: 聞得大君：琉球王国最高神女
items.desc_en: The highest-ranking priestess in the Ryukyu Kingdom's dual governance system (king for secular, kikoe-ogimi for sacred). Usually the king's sister or close female relative. Her investiture ceremony (oaraori) was held at Sefa Utaki. Embodied the onarigami belief in the spiritual superiority of sister
items.desc_ja: 琉球王国の政教二重統治における最高神女。通常国王の姉妹または近親女性。就任儀礼「御新下り」は斎場御嶽で挙行。姉妹の霊的優位性（おなり神）を体現。

items.title_en: Yuta: Spirit Mediums and Divination Practitioners
items.title_ja: ユタ：霊媒師と占卜実践者
items.desc_en: Unofficial spirit mediums (predominantly women) who provide divination, spiritual counseling, and healing. Unlike noro (state-appointed), yuta operate independently. Called through kamidaari (spiritual illness/calling). Services include ancestor communication, fortune telling (sanjinsou), and identi
items.desc_ja: 公式任命ではない民間霊媒師（主に女性）。占い、霊的相談、治療を行う。カミダーリー（神障り/霊的覚醒）を経て就く。祖先交信、三世相占い、霊障の原因特定を行う。

items.title_en: Kamidaari: Spiritual Calling and Initiatory Illness of Yuta
items.title_ja: カミダーリー：ユタの霊的召命と通過儀礼的病
items.desc_en: The involuntary spiritual awakening experienced by those destined to become yuta. Manifests as physical and mental distress that conventional medicine cannot cure. The afflicted person must accept their calling and undergo training with an established yuta to achieve resolution and develop their spi
items.desc_ja: ユタになる運命の者が経験する不随意的霊的覚醒。通常の医療では治せない身体的・精神的苦痛として現れる。師匠ユタの下での修行を経て解消される通過的危機。

items.title_en: Onarigami: Spiritual Power of Sisters over Brothers
items.title_ja: おなり神：姉妹の兄弟に対する霊的守護力
items.desc_en: Fundamental Ryukyuan belief that sisters possess innate spiritual power (shiji) to protect their brothers, especially during sea voyages. The concept underlies the entire female-centered religious system: the king needed the kikoe-ogimi's spiritual authority, and every man needed his sister's prayer
items.desc_ja: 姉妹が兄弟を守護する生来の霊力（セジ）を持つという琉球の根本的信仰。女性中心の宗教体系全体の基盤。国王は聞得大君の霊的権威を、男性は姉妹の祈りを必要とした。

items.desc_en: Sacred initiation ceremonies designating women as Noro priestesses, involving ritual purification, spiritual possession confirmation, and community recognition. Candidates undergo rigorous training in Okinawan religious protocols and ancestral veneration practices.
items.desc_ja: ノロとして女性を指定する神聖な就任式典。儀式的浄化、霊的憑依確認、地域社会の認可を含む。候補者は沖縄宗教儀礼と祖先祭祀の厳格な訓練を受ける。

items.title_ja: ユタの占術と霊的交信
items.desc_en: Mediumship practice where Yuta communicate with ancestral spirits and supernatural entities to diagnose illnesses, reveal hidden truths, and provide spiritual guidance. Uses trance states, interpretive utterances, and intuitive insights accumulated through decades of experience.
items.desc_ja: ユタが祖霊や超自然的存在と交信し、病気を診断し、隠された真実を明かし、精神的指導を提供する仲介実践。トランス状態、解釈的発言、数十年の経験で蓄積された直感的洞察を使用。

items.title_ja: カミダアリ神的憑依現象
items.desc_en: Spiritual phenomenon where deities or ancestral spirits possess practitioners' bodies during religious ceremonies. Kamidaari enables direct divine communication, transcendent experiences, and transmission of sacred knowledge. Essential to Noro and Yuta ritual authority and spiritual validation.
items.desc_ja: 宗教儀式中にヌシ神や祖霊が実践者の身体に憑依する精神現象。カミダアリは直接的な神的交信、超越的経験、聖なる知識の伝達を可能にする。ノロとユタの儀式的権威と精神的検証に不可欠。

items.title_ja: オナリガミ女性祖先神化
items.desc_en: Spiritual belief system elevating deceased female ancestors to divine status as protective deities. Onarigami embodies matriarchal spiritual authority, household guardianship, and generational wisdom transmission. Central to Okinawan religious identity and female-centered cosmology.
items.desc_ja: 故人の女性祖先を保護神としての神的地位に昇格させる精神信仰体系。オナリガミは母系的精神的権威、家庭の守護、世代的叡智の伝達を具現化する。沖縄宗教的アイデンティティと女性中心的宇宙観の中核。

items.title_ja: キコエオオギミ最高司祭女性権威
items.desc_en: Legendary supreme priestess embodying ultimate spiritual authority in Okinawan religious hierarchy. Kikoe-ogimi represents the highest level of ritual expertise, direct divine connection, and political-spiritual leadership. Symbol of female religious empowerment and sacred governance.
items.desc_ja: 沖縄宗教階級の最高精神的権威を体現する伝説的最高司祭女性。キコエオオギミは最高レベルの儀式専門知識、直接的神的結合、政治的・精神的指導権を代表する。女性宗教的権能と聖なる統治の象徴。

items.title_ja: ノロの季節的浄化儀式
items.desc_en: Annual ritual cycles led by Noro priestesses purifying communities, households, and sacred spaces. Ceremonies aligned with agricultural calendars, lunar phases, and traditional festivals. Ensures spiritual cleanliness, divine favor, and protection from malevolent supernatural forces.
items.desc_ja: ノロ司祭女性が主導する年間儀式サイクルは、コミュニティ、家庭、聖なる空間を浄化する。儀式は農業暦、月相、伝統的祭礼に適合させる。精神的清潔さ、神的恩寵、悪意ある超自然的力からの保護を確保する。

items.title_en: Yuta Healing Diagnosis Through Spirit Consultation
items.title_ja: ユタの霊的相談による治療診断
items.desc_en: Diagnostic methodology where Yuta consult spirits to identify illness causes—psychological, spiritual, or karmic. Combines intuitive interpretation, ancestral messaging, and supernatural insight. Provides holistic understanding of health conditions integrating physical and metaphysical dimensions.
items.desc_ja: ユタが霊に相談して病気の原因を特定する診断方法論―心理的、精神的、または因果的。直感的解釈、祖先メッセージング、超自然的洞察を組み合わせる。身体的および形而上学的次元を統合する健康状態の全体的理解を提供する。

items.title_ja: ノロの政治宗教的二元権力体制
items.desc_en: Historical system where Noro priestesses wielded both spiritual and administrative authority over communities. Integrated religious ritual leadership with economic management, dispute resolution, and political decision-making. Reflects Okinawan female-centered governance structures.
items.desc_ja: ノロ司祭女性がコミュニティに対して精神的および管理的権威を揮った歴史的体制。宗教的儀式指導を経済的管理、紛争解決、政治的意思決定と統合した。沖縄女性中心的統治構造を反映している。

items.title_ja: ユタのトランス状態発話解釈
items.desc_en: Specialized skill interpreting mystical utterances and symbolic communications occurring during Yuta trance possession. Requires linguistic knowledge, spiritual sensitivity, and cultural symbolism understanding. Practitioners decode divine messages addressing personal, familial, and communal concern
items.desc_ja: ユタトランス憑依中に発生する神秘的発話と象徴的コミュニケーションを解釈する専門技能。言語知識、精神的感性、文化的象徴主義理解が必要。実践者は個人的、家族的、共同的懸念に対処する神聖なメッセージをデコードする。

items.title_ja: ノロ仲介儀式を通じた祖先崇拝
items.desc_en: Ceremonial practices where Noro priestesses facilitate communication between living communities and ancestral spirits. Honors deceased family members, maintains spiritual kinship bonds, and ensures ancestral blessings. Integrates household religion with communal spiritual obligations.
items.desc_ja: ノロ司祭女性が生きているコミュニティと祖霊の間のコミュニケーションを促進する儀式実践。故人の家族メンバーを敬い、精神的血族結合を維持し、祖先の祝福を確保する。家庭宗教を共同的精神的義務と統合する。

items.desc_en: Sacred ceremonies marking the formal installation of new Noro priestesses, involving ritual purification, divine possession recognition, and community acknowledgment of spiritual authority transferred through generational lineage.
items.desc_ja: 新しいノロ神女の正式な就任を示す神聖な儀式。儀式的浄化、神の所有の認識、世代間の精神的権威の譲受を含む。

items.title_ja: ユタの診断的霊的交信
items.desc_en: Yuta mediums employ trance states to communicate with ancestral and spiritual entities, diagnosing illnesses, family misfortunes, and spiritual imbalances through direct spirit consultation and channeled messages.
items.desc_ja: ユタ媒介者がトランス状態を使用して祖先と精神的実体と通信し、精神的相談と通信されたメッセージを通じて疾患を診断する。

items.title_ja: カミダーリー聖なる通路の描定
items.desc_en: Spiritual practice identifying and consecrating sacred pathways where deities traverse landscapes, involving landscape reading, spirit presence recognition, and ceremonial establishment of protected spiritual corridors.
items.desc_ja: 神が景観を通過する聖なる通路を特定して奉献する精神的実践。景観読解、精神の存在認識、保護された精神的回廊の儀式的確立を含む。

items.title_ja: オナリガミ保護神との関係
items.desc_en: Onarigami are protective deities associated with female spiritual power, with Noro priestesses maintaining sacred relationships through rituals, prayers, and offerings ensuring community welfare and divine blessing.
items.desc_ja: オナリガミは女性の精神的力に関連した保護神。ノロ神女は儀式、祈り、供え物を通じて聖なる関係を維持し、共同体の幸福と神の祝福を確保する。

items.title_ja: キコエオオギミ神の声の解釈
items.desc_en: The highest priestess rank interpreting divine messages through sound and spiritual resonance, translating cosmic consciousness into communal guidance, maintaining cosmic-human balance through careful vocalization and ritualized speech.
items.desc_ja: 音と精神的共鳴を通じて神のメッセージを解釈する最高司祭。宇宙意識を共同体的導きに翻訳し、慎重な発音と儀式化された言葉を通じて宇宙と人間のバランスを維持する。

items.desc_en: Yuta mediums facilitate ancestral communications during household rituals, establishing spiritual connections between living families and deceased relatives, addressing unresolved family conflicts and honoring ancestral spirits.
items.desc_ja: ユタ媒介者は家庭儀式中に祖先の通信を促進し、生きている家族と亡くなった親戚の間の精神的つながりを確立し、未解決の家族紛争に対処する。

items.title_ja: ユタの疾病原因診断法
items.desc_en: Specialized diagnostic techniques where Yuta mediums identify whether illnesses stem from spiritual possession, ancestral anger, broken taboos, or malevolent spirits, determining appropriate healing interventions and ritual remedies.
items.desc_ja: ユタ媒介者が疾病が精神的所有、祖先の怒り、禁止された破壊、または悪意のある精神から生じるかを識別する特殊な診断技術。適切な治療介入と儀式的救済を決定する。

items.title_ja: ノロ収穫祭祝福プロトコル
items.desc_en: Noro priestesses lead harvest thanksgiving ceremonies blessing crops and agricultural communities, performing sacred dances, offering gratitude to agricultural deities, ensuring future fertility and village prosperity.
items.desc_ja: ノロ神女は作物と農業共同体を祝福する収穫感謝儀式を主導。聖なるダンスを実施し、農業神に感謝を捧げ、将来の肥沃度と村の繁栄を確保する。

items.title_ja: ユタの精神的所有認識の兆候
items.desc_en: Traditional knowledge of identifying legitimate spirit possession versus psychological disturbance, involving symptom analysis, behavioral patterns, and validation criteria determining authentic mediumship within community spiritual framework.
items.desc_ja: 正当な精神的所有と心理的障害の区別を特定する伝統的知識。症状分析、行動パターン、共同体の精神的枠組み内での本物の媒介性を決定する検証基準を含む。

items.title_en: Sefa Utaki: Supreme Sacred Site of the Ryukyu Kingdom
items.title_ja: 斎場御嶽：琉球王国最高聖地
items.desc_en: The most sacred site in the Ryukyu Kingdom, located in Nanjo City. Used for the coronation ceremony (oaraori) of the Kikoe-ogimi (supreme priestess). Contains six ibi (sacred spaces) including the Sangui, a triangular rock formation opening toward Kudaka Island. UNESCO World Heritage Site since 2000
items.desc_ja: 南城市に位置する琉球王国最高の聖地。聞得大君の就任儀礼「御新下り」が行われた。三庫理をはじめ6つの拝所を持ち、久高島を望む。2000年世界遺産登録。

items.title_en: Kudaka Island: Island of the Gods and Origin of Agriculture
items.title_ja: 久高島：神の島と農耕発祥の地
items.desc_en: Small island east of the Okinawa mainland considered the most sacred place in Ryukyuan cosmology. Believed to be where the deity Amamikiyo descended from heaven to create the Ryukyu Islands. The Izaiho ceremony (last held 1978) was a 12-yearly initiation of priestesses. Origin myth of rice and grain
items.desc_ja: 沖縄本島東方の小島。琉球神話でアマミキヨが天から降り立った聖地。イザイホー（最終催行1978年）は12年に一度の神女就任儀礼。五穀発祥伝承の地。

items.title_ja: ニライカナイ：海の彼方の理想郷
items.desc_en: Central concept in Ryukyuan cosmology referring to a paradise beyond the sea from which deities, ancestors, and blessings originate. Prayers at coastal utaki are directed toward nirai kanai. The concept shapes funeral orientation, agricultural prayers, and the spiritual geography of village layouts.
items.desc_ja: 琉球世界観の中核概念。海の彼方にある神々と祖先の楽土。沿岸の御嶽での祈りはニライカナイに向けられる。葬送・農耕祈願・集落配置の精神的地理を規定。

items.title_ja: ウマチー：集落農耕儀礼
items.desc_en: Three annual agricultural festivals (February, March, and June by lunar calendar) conducted at village utaki. Involves offering of first fruits, prayers for abundant harvest, and communal feasting. The noro priestess leads the ceremonies while village men prepare offerings.
items.desc_ja: 旧暦2月・3月・6月に御嶽で行われる3回の農耕儀礼。初穂の奉納、豊穣祈願、共同饗宴を含む。ノロが祭祀を主導し、男性が供物を準備する。

items.title_en: Ungami Festival: Sea God Festival and Maritime Prayers
items.title_ja: ウンガミ祭：海神祭と海の祈り
items.desc_en: Annual festival honoring sea deities, held in coastal villages during the 7th month of the lunar calendar. Features dragon boat races (haarii), prayers for safe voyages and abundant catches. The noro leads prayers at the shore, invoking nirai kanai for the community's maritime prosperity.
items.desc_ja: 旧暦7月に沿岸集落で行われる海神を祀る年中行事。爬龍船競漕、航海安全・豊漁祈願を含む。ノロが浜辺でニライカナイに向けて祈願を主導する。

items.title_en: Shiimi: Qingming Tomb Visiting and Ancestor Veneration
items.title_ja: シーミー（清明祭）：墓参と祖先崇拝
items.desc_en: Okinawan adaptation of the Chinese Qingming festival, held in April. Families gather at munchuu (lineage) tombs for picnics with ancestors, offering uchinsaku (重箱料理) containing 9 dishes. The event reinforces patrilineal kinship bonds and ancestral connections across generations.
items.desc_ja: 中国の清明節を沖縄化した4月の行事。門中墓に親族が集い、重箱料理（ウチンサク）9品を供え祖先と食事する。父系親族の絆と世代間の祖先接続を強化する。

items.title_en: Hinukan: Hearth Deity and Household Spirituality
items.title_ja: ヒヌカン（火の神）：竈の神と家庭霊性
items.desc_en: The hearth deity worshipped in every traditional Okinawan household. The female head of household maintains the hinukan altar near the kitchen, offering incense and prayers on the 1st and 15th of each lunar month. Hinukan mediates between the household and the village utaki system.
items.desc_ja: 全ての伝統的沖縄家庭で祀られる竈の神。女性家長が台所近くの香炉を管理し、旧暦1日・15日に線香を供える。家庭と集落御嶽体系を媒介する。

items.title_en: Haarii Dragon Boat Race: Maritime Ritual Competition
items.title_ja: ハーリー（爬龍船競漕）：海の儀礼競争
items.desc_en: Dragon boat racing held annually in fishing villages, especially prominent in Naha (May 4th lunar calendar) and Itoman. Three boats representing different community groups race to ensure abundant fishing and safe seas. Combines Chinese dragon boat tradition with Ryukyuan maritime spirituality.
items.desc_ja: 漁業集落で年1回開催される爬龍船競漕。那覇（旧暦5月4日）・糸満で盛大。3隻の舟が豊漁と航海安全を祈って競う。中国龍舟と琉球海洋霊性の融合。

items.title_en: Tsunahiki: Great Tug of War Ritual
items.desc_en: Massive tug-of-war events held across Okinawa, most famously in Naha (October) with a 200m, 40-ton rope. The east (agi) and west (iri) sides represent cosmic dualities. The rope's interlocking male and female ends symbolize fertility. Winners predict agricultural fortune for the coming year.
items.desc_ja: 沖縄各地で行われる大綱引き。那覇（10月）は全長200m・40トンの綱で最大級。東（アギ）と西（イリ）が宇宙的二元性を表す。綱の雌雄結合が豊穣を象徴。

items.title_en: Obotsu Kagura: Celestial Realm in Ryukyuan Cosmology
items.title_ja: オボツカグラ：琉球世界観の天上界
items.desc_en: The celestial counterpart to nirai kanai in Ryukyuan cosmology. While nirai kanai lies beyond the sea, obotsu kagura represents the heavenly realm where deities reside. The dual structure of above/below, mountain/sea shapes the spiritual geography of utaki placement and prayer orientation.
items.desc_ja: ニライカナイの天上対応概念。海の彼方がニライカナイなら、天上界がオボツカグラ。上下・山海の二重構造が御嶽配置と祈りの方向性を規定する琉球宇宙論。

items.desc_en: Sacred protocols for purifying utaki groves before major ceremonies. Practitioners perform water ablutions, salt offerings, and verbal incantations to prepare spiritual spaces and remove spiritual pollution from the sacred landscape.
items.desc_ja: 大祭前の御嶽浄化儀式。水による禊、塩供え、言葉による清めで霊的汚れを除去し聖域を整える伝統的儀礼。

items.desc_en: Hereditary system of female noro priestesses serving utaki sanctuaries. Daughters inherit sacred duties, ritual knowledge, and spiritual authority from mothers through family lineage, maintaining continuous spiritual guardianship of sacred groves.
items.desc_ja: 母から娘へ継承される女性神官制度。御嶽の霊的守護者として代々受け継がれる聖なる職務と儀礼知識。

items.desc_en: Traditional dragon boat racing ceremony combining religious worship with communal labor. Teams from different districts compete during festival celebrations, honoring ocean deities and celebrating maritime heritage through synchronized rowing and spiritual invocation.
items.desc_ja: 竜舟競争を通じた地域共同の宗教儀式。海の神々への礼拝と同期した漕ぎで海洋伝統を祝う祭礼。

items.desc_en: Spiritual cosmology embedded in tsunahiki rope-pulling contests representing cosmic balance between opposing forces. The ritual demonstrates terrestrial harmony with celestial order through synchronized community participation and symbolic struggle imagery.
items.desc_ja: 綱引きに込められた宇宙的均衡思想。対立する力のバランスを象徴する儀式で天地秩序との調和を表現。

items.title_ja: ニライカナイ航海信仰
items.desc_en: Navigational spirituality referencing the mythological paradise island Nirai-Kanai located beyond the horizon. Fishermen and sailors observe traditional directional practices and protective rituals when traveling toward sacred ocean realms.
items.desc_ja: 地平線の彼方の理想郷ニライカナイへの航海信仰。漁師による方位観察と海上での保護儀式。

items.desc_en: Traditional methods of marking utaki sacred grove boundaries using natural materials and spiritual symbols. Practitioners place sacred stones, ritual ropes, and symbolic markers to delineate holy spaces and protect them from secular intrusion.
items.desc_ja: 石、縄、シンボルで御嶽の聖域を画定する伝統的方法。俗界からの侵入防止と聖域保護。

items.desc_en: Elaborate ritual calendar prescribing specific visitation times to different utaki sanctuaries throughout the year. Communities observe precise seasonal sequences of prayers and offerings synchronized with agricultural cycles and celestial observations.
items.desc_ja: 年間を通じた御嶽参拝の厳密な時系列。農業周期と天体観察に同期した祈りと供え。

items.desc_en: Specialized vocal chanting performed during haarii boat races to invoke ocean deities and marine spirits. Rhythmic incantations synchronized with rowing motions create spiritual communion with water realms while energizing competitive team performance.
items.desc_ja: ハーリー中の海神を呼ぶ唱え。漕ぎのリズムに同期した詞で海の精霊との霊的交感を造成。

items.title_ja: 綱引き性別補完的役割
items.desc_en: Tug-of-war ceremony employing distinct gender roles where men and women occupy separate rope sections with complementary spiritual functions. The dual-gender structure represents cosmological duality and maintains social harmony through prescribed participation patterns.
items.desc_ja: 男女が異なる綱の位置を占める補完的役割分担。二元的宇宙観と社会調和を表現する構造。

items.title_ja: ニライカナイ贈与相互性
items.desc_en: Reciprocal exchange system with the mythological Nirai-Kanai realm where offerings are given expecting spiritual blessings in return. Communities perform ritualized gift-giving to invisible divine entities maintaining cosmic reciprocity and seasonal abundance.
items.desc_ja: 理想郷への供え物と霊的祝福の相互交換制度。宇宙的相互性と季節的豊穣を維持する。

items.desc_en: Sacred grove canopy purification rituals performed to maintain spiritual sanctity. Practitioners climb ancient trees within utaki sites, conducting offerings and prayers to strengthen celestial connections and ensure forest vitality.
items.desc_ja: 御嶽内の樹冠を浄化する儀礼。古木に登り、天との結びつきを強め、森の活力を保つための供え物と祈りを捧げる。

items.title_ja: ニライカナイ方位航法
items.desc_en: Traditional navigation knowledge using nirai-kanai directional markers. Fishermen and maritime specialists employ sacred directions toward the otherworldly realm to guide fishing expeditions and ensure safe passage.
items.desc_ja: ニライカナイの方位を用いた航海知識。漁師が他界への聖なる方向を使い、漁へと導き安全な航海を保証する。

items.title_ja: ハーリー豊収祈願儀式
items.desc_en: Communal haarii rope-pulling rituals combining physical collective action with spiritual invocation. Teams pull sacred ropes while chanting prayers, invoking bountiful harvests and community prosperity through synchronized effort.
items.desc_ja: 綱引きハーリーと精神的祈願を組み合わせた儀式。チームが聖なる綱を引きながら、豊かな収穫と共同体の繁栄を祈る。

items.desc_en: Tsunahiki tug-of-war with gender-complementary roles embodying cosmic balance. Male and female teams represent opposing sacred forces; their struggle symbolizes seasonal transitions and agricultural renewal through complementary opposition.
items.desc_ja: 男女の役割分担による綱引き。相対する聖なる力を表現し、季節の転換と農業の更新を象徴する。

items.title_ja: 御嶽での先祖霊との交通
items.desc_en: Ritual communication with ancestral spirits within utaki sacred spaces. Female priestesses facilitate dialogue between living communities and deceased forebears, maintaining genealogical continuity and spiritual protection.
items.desc_ja: 御嶽での先祖霊との対話儀礼。女性聖職者が生者と故人の交通を仲介し、系統継続性と精神的保護を保つ。

items.desc_en: Specialized knowledge of medicinal and ritual plants within utaki groves. Practitioners identify healing herbs and sacred vegetation for ceremonial use, maintaining ecological and spiritual balance within protected forest spaces.
items.desc_ja: 御嶽内の薬用植物と儀礼植物の知識。癒しの草や聖なる植物を特定し、保護された森の生態と精神的バランスを保つ。

items.desc_en: Astronomical observation methods determining ceremonial timing. Elders track solar positions relative to utaki landmarks, calculating seasonal ceremonies and agricultural festivals aligned with celestial cycles.
items.desc_ja: 御嶽を基準点とした太陽位置観測。年長者が季節儀礼と農業祭祀の時期を決定し、天体周期と整合させる。

items.title_ja: ハーリー掛け声調整技法
items.desc_en: Specialized chanting protocols coordinating haarii rope-pulling teams. Leaders employ rhythmic vocalizations synchronizing collective force, embedding prayers and community identity within participatory soundscapes.
items.desc_ja: ハーリーチーム調整の掛け声技法。指導者がリズム的な声で集団力を同期させ、祈りと共同体アイデンティティを組み込む。

items.title_ja: ニライカナイからの季節来訪期待
items.desc_en: Interpretive knowledge of seasonal resource cycles originating from nirai-kanai realm. Communities anticipate fish runs, wind patterns, and plant growth based on sacred directional beliefs and observational experience.
items.desc_ja: ニライカナイ由来の季節資源循環の解釈。魚群、風向、植生成長を聖なる信念と観察経験で予測する。

items.desc_en: Interpretive frameworks assigning cosmic significance to tsunahiki tug-of-war outcomes. Victory or defeat symbolizes agricultural success, weather patterns, and spiritual balance; elders explain results to community members.
items.desc_ja: 綱引きの勝敗を宇宙的意味として解釈する知識体系。勝敗は農業成功、気象、精神的バランスを象徴する。

items.title_en: Munchuu System: Patrilineal Descent Groups and Genealogy
items.title_ja: 門中制度：父系出自集団と系図
items.desc_en: Patrilineal kinship organization (munchuu) that structures Okinawan social life. Each munchuu maintains genealogical records, shares an ancestral tomb, and conducts collective rituals (especially Shiimi/Qingming). The system defines inheritance, marriage eligibility, and mutual aid obligations. Firs
items.desc_ja: 沖縄社会を構造化する父系親族組織。各門中は系図を維持し、先祖の墓を共有し、シーミー等の集合的祭祀を行う。相続・婚姻適格・相互扶助義務を規定。長男相続が伝統。

items.title_en: Moai: Rotating Credit Association and Mutual Aid
items.title_ja: 模合（モアイ）：頼母子講と相互扶助
items.desc_en: Traditional rotating savings and credit association where members contribute a fixed amount monthly and take turns receiving the pooled sum. Functions as both financial instrument and social bonding mechanism. Modern moai groups may focus on drinking/socializing rather than financial needs. Reflects
items.desc_ja: 月例で固定額を出し合い、順番に総額を受け取る伝統的頼母子講。金融手段であると同時に社会的結合装置として機能。現代では飲み会・親睦目的の模合も多い。ゆいまーる精神を反映。

items.title_en: Yuimaru: Reciprocal Labor Exchange and Community Cooperation
items.title_ja: ゆいまーる：相互扶助労働交換
items.desc_en: Traditional system of reciprocal labor exchange where community members help each other with harvests, house building, and celebrations without monetary compensation. The accumulated social debt creates networks of obligation that bind communities together. The concept extends to emotional support a
items.desc_ja: 収穫・建築・祝い事で金銭を介さず互いに労働を提供する相互扶助体系。蓄積された社会的負債が共同体を結ぶ義務のネットワークを形成。感情的支援や危機対応にも拡張される概念。

items.desc_en: Extended family organization tracing patrilineal descent, managing collective property and obligations. Members share ancestor veneration, mutual aid, and ceremonial responsibilities across generations within structured hierarchical framework.
items.desc_ja: 父系血統に基づく拡大家族組織。共有財産管理と相互扶助義務を担い、祖先祭祀と儀式責任を世代間で継承する階層的構造。

items.desc_en: Community-based rotating savings and credit association where members contribute fixed amounts monthly, with payouts distributed among participants sequentially. Strengthens economic cooperation and social bonds through mutual financial support mechanisms.
items.desc_ja: 成員が毎月定額を拠出し、順番に全額を受け取る相互扶助の金銭制度。経済協力と社会結合を強化し、村落単位で実践される。

items.desc_en: Reciprocal labor cooperation system where community members assist each other in agricultural work, construction, and major household tasks without monetary compensation. Reinforces village solidarity and ensures resource redistribution across economic strata.
items.desc_ja: 農作業や建築、家事労働を無償で相互扶助する労働交換制度。村落共同体の結合を強化し、経済的困窮家庭を支援する仕組み。

items.desc_en: Traditional village administrative organization managing collective land use, resource distribution, and conflict resolution through consensus-based decision-making. Elders and community leaders maintain social order and enforce customary regulations.
items.desc_ja: 共有地利用と資源配分を管理し、合意形成に基づく紛争解決を行う村落行政。長老と指導者が慣習規範を維持する。

items.desc_en: Formal ceremonial procession accompanying bride between munchuu residences, involving ritual music, symbolic dress, and community witnessing. Establishes social bonds between families and publicly legitimizes marital union within extended kinship networks.
items.desc_ja: 新婦を新郎家の門中へ導く儀礼的行列。音楽、装束、親族参加により社会的結合を確立し、婚姻を公式に認証する。

items.desc_en: Munchuu-organized funeral arrangements coordinating mourning periods, ritualistic preparations, and commemorative obligations. Distributes ceremonial responsibilities among family members and manages ancestral veneration rites spanning multiple days.
items.desc_ja: 門中による葬儀組織。喪の期間、儀式準備、追悼義務を調整し、複数日間の祖先祭祀責任を配分する。

items.desc_en: Patrilineal inheritance system granting eldest son preferential access to family properties and ceremonial leadership roles. Maintains economic stability and preserves ritual authority within extended family lineages across generational succession.
items.desc_ja: 長男が家督と儀式指導権を相続する父系制。経済基盤を維持し、門中内の儀礼権限を世代継承する。

items.desc_en: Community established protocols determining moai group membership based on economic reliability, social standing, and neighborhood residence. Ensures financial trustworthiness and strengthens localized community networks through deliberate participant selection.
items.desc_ja: 経済的信頼性と社会的地位に基づく模合成員の選定。近隣住民による信用構築で、地域結合を強化する基準。

items.desc_en: Village-level system allocating arable land plots to households based on family size, economic need, and historical usage rights. Prevents wealth concentration, ensures food security, and regulates resource access through collective stewardship principles.
items.desc_ja: 家族規模と経済需要に基づき農地を配分する村落制度。富の集中を防ぎ、食糧安全保障と資源平等利用を維持する。

items.desc_en: Specialized moai group established specifically for funeral expense mutual aid, allowing members to receive financial assistance during death-related ceremonial costs. Ensures families can conduct proper rituals without economic hardship.
items.desc_ja: 葬儀費用支援専門の模合。成員が葬儀時に経済援助を受け、儀式的負担を軽減し、円滑な追悼儀礼を実現する。

items.desc_en: Systematic documentation of family lineage within munchuu groups, recording genealogical relationships across generations. Eldest members maintain detailed records of births, marriages, and deaths to preserve family history and inheritance rights.
items.desc_ja: 門中内の家系図を系統的に記録・管理する慣行。出生、婚姻、死亡を記録し、複数世代にわたる家族関係を保存。相続権と家族の歴史を維持する。

items.desc_en: Monthly gathering where community members contribute equal amounts to a collective fund, with one member receiving the total amount each meeting. This informal rotating credit system supports major household expenses like weddings, home construction, and emergencies.
items.desc_ja: 毎月集合して等額を拠出し、回転的に総額を受け取る金融システム。結婚式、家屋建設、緊急費用などの大規模支出を相互に支援する。

items.desc_en: Reciprocal labor-sharing arrangement where community members assist each other with agricultural work, construction, and household tasks without monetary compensation. Records of work exchanges ensure balanced participation and future obligation fulfillment.
items.desc_ja: 農作業、建設、家事などで相互に無償労働を提供する慣行。労働交換記録により均衡の取れた参加と将来の義務履行を確保する。

items.title_ja: 島村落の結婚式寄付制度
items.desc_en: Organized village practice where households contribute money and labor resources to support wedding ceremonies. Contributions are recorded and reciprocated when families later host their own weddings, ensuring community-wide economic participation.
items.desc_ja: 結婚式を支援するため村落内の各戸が金銭と労働を提供する制度。寄付は記録され、後に自家の結婚式時に報いられる。

items.title_ja: 葬儀における門中の集団責任
items.desc_en: Extended family members and munchuu groups share funeral responsibilities including body preparation, grave maintenance, and ritual observance. Leadership roles rotate among senior members, and cost-sharing ensures financial burden distribution.
items.desc_ja: 葬儀の準備、埋葬地管理、儀式执行を門中構成員が共同で担当。指導的役割は年長者間で交代し、経費は分散される。

items.title_ja: 模合への加入儀礼と選定
items.desc_en: Traditional selection criteria and initiation protocols for moai membership, ensuring participants are trustworthy and financially stable. Acceptance requires village elder approval and consensus, with formal ceremonies marking membership entrance.
items.desc_ja: 模合加入者の選定基準と入会儀礼。信頼性と経済的安定性を確認し、村落長老の承認と合意により加入。入会時に形式的な儀礼を実施。

items.desc_en: Formal procedures for resolving disputes within extended family structures, using mediation by senior munchuu members. Emphasis on consensus-building, face-saving, and restoration of harmony rather than adversarial resolution.
items.desc_ja: 門中内の紛争を年長者の仲介により解決する手続き。合意形成、面目保全、調和の回復を重視し、対立的解決は避ける。

items.title_ja: 島村落の共同食事の運営
items.desc_en: Coordinated village practice organizing collective meals for major events and seasonal celebrations. Households take turns providing ingredients and labor, with menus adapted to available local resources and seasonal harvests.
items.desc_ja: 大きな行事と季節の祝いのため村落で共同食事を企画。各戸が順番に食材と労働を提供。地域の資源と季節の収穫に合わせた献立。

items.title_ja: ゆいまるによる子育て支援
items.desc_en: Collective childcare practice where village women cooperatively supervise and nurture children during community work periods. Shared responsibility reduces individual family burden while maintaining cultural transmission of values and social norms.
items.desc_ja: 村落の女性が協力して共同作業中に子どもを監督・養育する慣行。個別家庭の負担軽減と文化的価値の伝承を実現。

items.desc_en: Coordinated annual schedule of ancestor worship ceremonies observed by extended family members. Regular observances include birth anniversaries of significant ancestors, seasonal festivals, and collective tomb visits strengthening munchuu unity.
items.desc_ja: 門中構成員が守る祖先祭祀の年間日程。著名な祖先の誕生記念日、季節祭、集団墓参により門中の結束を強化。

items.desc_en: Formal procedures for transferring munchuu leadership authority to designated heir. Involves ritual acknowledgment by family members, property documentation, and ancestral veneration ceremonies ensuring continuity of lineage responsibilities and social standing.
items.desc_ja: 門中の当主から次期指導者への権威移譲の儀式的手続き。家族成員の儀礼的認可、財産記録、祖先祭祀を含み、血統の責任と社会的地位の継続性を保証する。

items.title_ja: 模合の緊急食糧援助制度
items.desc_en: Mutual aid mechanism within moai rotating savings groups to provide emergency food supplies during natural disasters or family hardship. Members contribute surplus harvests and preserved foods into communal reserves distributed during crises.
items.desc_ja: 模合の相互扶助メカニズムで、災害や家族困窮時に食糧供給を提供。成員が余剰農産物と保存食を共同備蓄に寄与し、危機時に配分される。

items.title_en: Yuimaru Labor Exchange for House Construction
items.title_ja: 結いまるによる住宅建設労働交換
items.desc_en: Community-organized collective labor system where shima village residents pool workforce for house building projects. Participants trade working hours without monetary compensation, strengthening social bonds while reducing individual construction costs significantly.
items.desc_ja: 島の住民が住宅建設の労働力を集約する地域組織的なシステム。参加者が報酬なしで労働時間を交換し、社会結合を強化しつつ個別建設費用を大幅削減。

items.desc_en: Detailed genealogical calendar maintained by munchuu members documenting death anniversaries and memorial observance dates. Ensures all ancestors receive appropriate ritual remembrance, coordinating family gatherings for spiritual obligations across generations.
items.desc_ja: 門中成員により保管される詳細な世系暦。死亡忌日と追悼観察日を記録し、全祖先が適切な儀礼を受けることを保証し、世代間の精神的義務のための家族集会を調整。

items.title_ja: 模合の結婚式費用分担制度
items.desc_en: Rotating moai group members contribute fixed monetary amounts toward member wedding expenses. Contributions cycle through members, providing substantial financial support for elaborate traditional Okinawan wedding ceremonies and celebration preparations.
items.desc_ja: 模合成員が結婚式費用に定額を分担。寄与が成員間で循環し、精巧な沖縄伝統結婚式と祝賀準備のための実質的な財政支援を提供。

items.title_ja: 島村の葬儀調整ネットワーク
items.desc_en: Integrated village system organizing collective funeral responsibilities across households. Designates specific members for food preparation, grave maintenance, ritual coordination, and mourning period management, distributing labor equitably among all residents.
items.desc_ja: 世帯間の葬儀責任を組織する統合村システム。食事準備、墓地保守、儀礼調整、喪の期間管理の特定成員を指定し、全住民間で労働を公平に配分。

items.title_ja: 結いまるの季節農業労働班
items.desc_en: Flexible yuimaru teams organize rotating seasonal agricultural labor across village farmlands. Members work collectively on planting, weeding, and harvesting cycles, with task rotation ensuring equitable participation and knowledge transfer of farming techniques.
items.desc_ja: 柔軟な結いまるチームが農地間の季節農業労働を組織。成員が播種、除草、収穫サイクルで共同作業し、任務交換が公平な参加と農業技術知識の伝承を保証。

items.title_ja: 門中の結婚式儀礼専門家
items.desc_en: Designated munchuu elders serving as wedding ceremony experts, directing ritual sequences, managing guest seating arrangements, and ensuring proper ancestral acknowledgment. Knowledge transmitted through family lineage observation and direct instruction over decades.
items.desc_ja: 結婚式儀礼の専門家として機能する指定門中長老。儀式順序の指揮、客席配置の管理、適切な祖先認識を保証。知識は数十年間の家族血統観察と直接指導を通じて伝承。

items.title_ja: 模合の葬儀費用循環基金
items.desc_en: Moai members maintain rotating funeral fund reserves, providing significant financial assistance when member households experience deaths. Contributions standardized, distributions managed transparently, alleviating economic burden of expensive funeral preparations and ceremonies.
items.desc_ja: 模合成員が葬儀基金循環準備金を保持。成員世帯が死亡時に多額の財政援助を提供。寄付は標準化、配分は透明に管理され、高額な葬儀準備と儀礼の経済的負担を軽減。

items.desc_en: Organized village teams performing annual maintenance of communal land boundaries, irrigation systems, and shared resources. Yuimaru-based work coordination ensures infrastructure protection, dispute prevention, and collective responsibility for village territorial integrity.
items.desc_ja: 共同土地境界、灌漑システム、共有資源の年次保守を実施する組織化村チーム。結いまるベースの作業調整が基盤保護、紛争防止、村領土完全性の集団責任を保証。
